Center: US National Nuclear Data Center, Brookhaven, USA
ref: Conf: Conf.on Nucl.Data for Sci.and Techn.,Gatlinburg 1994, p.179 (1994)
title: Measurements of (n,g) cross sections for very small stable and radioactive samples of interest to the s- and p-process

x4freetext: Measurements of (n,g) cross sections for very small
stable and radioactive samples of interest to the s-
and p-process

x4codes.shortRef: Conf: Conf.on Nucl.Data for Sci.and Techn.,Gatlinburg 1994, p.179 (1994)

x4codes.hlp: Los Alamos National Laboratory, NM, United States of America
Los Alamos National Laboratory, NM, United States of America

x4freetext: The Los Alamos Neutron Scattering
Center (LANSCE).
Spallation from proton + W.
A pair of deuterated benzene C6D6 scintillators.
Both the 191,193Ir samples were highly enriched.
191Ir and 193Ir were measured using 1 mg and 2 mg
samples, respectively. The samples consisted of
metallic powder which had been "super-glued" to 0.8
microm-thick aluminum backing foils.
The gamma-ray yield as a function of energy was
measured down to thermal energy and the measured
thermal cross sections were used to normalize the
yields to absolute cross sections. The total beam
time was about 1 day for each sample. The experiment
was performed on flight path 4 at LANSCE to
demonstrate the feasibility of measurements on very
small samples.

x4code.headerHelp: Error in value of quantity, defined under ERR-ANALYS

x4freetext: The uncertainties are 1-sigma in barns.
The data have not been corrected for self shielding
and multiple scattering. The 191Ir data have been
corrected for the small amount of 193Ir in the
enriched 191Ir sample and vice versa.
Approved by P.E.Koehler on August 21, 2025.
